TicketRestriction.Name: 1A
TicketRestriction.Easement:
The following easements apply on Greater Anglia services from Monday to Friday inclusive:

  • 08:20 Clacton-on Sea to London Liverpool Street service - from Clacton, Thorpe-le-Soken, Weeley, Great Bentley, Alresford, Wivenhoe and Hythe, to all destinations.
  • 08:01 Walton-on-the-Naze to London Liverpool Street service, from Walton, Frinton-on-Sea and Kirby Cross to all destinations.
  • 08:08 Harwich Town to Colchester service - from all stations to all destinations.
  • 09:02 London Liverpool Street to Ipswich service - from Liverpool Street and Stratford (London) to Chappel & Wakes Colne, Bures and Sudbury (Suffolk) only, changing at Marks Tey.
  • 09:18 London Liverpool Street to Clacton-on-Sea service - from Liverpool Street and Stratford (London) to Wivenhoe, Thorpe-le-Soken, Clacton-on-Sea, Kirby Cross, Frinton-on-Sea and Walton-on-the-Naze.
  • 09:28 London Liverpool Street to Cambridge North service, to all destinations.
  • 18:30 London Liverpool Street to Norwich service - from Liverpool Street to Derby Road (Ipswich), Trimley and Felixstowe only, changing at Ipswich.
TicketRestriction.SeasonalVariations:
School Holidays - Evening peak restrictions on return journeys from London Liverpool Street and Stratford (London) (which only apply to Ipswich and beyond) and from Liverpool Street and Tottenham Hale (which only apply to Brandon and beyond via Cambridge) are lifted during school holidays and half-term weeks. Note that restrictions from London Kings Cross are not affected and remain in place.

2024

Summer: Monday 22 July to Tuesday 3 September

Autumn Half-Term: Friday 25 October to Friday 1 November

Christmas and New Year: Monday 23 December to Friday 3 January 2025.

2025

Half-Term: Monday 17 to Friday 21 February

Easter: Monday 7 to Tuesday 22 April (Friday 18 and Monday 21 April are public holidays)

Half-Term: Tuesday 27 to Friday 30 May (Monday 26 May is a public holiday)

Summer: Starts 23 July

All dates are inclusive and are taken from the Norfolk County Council website.

Restriction.DetailsOutward: Valid on any train timed to arrive at London Liverpool Street (or London Kings Cross or London St Pancras International from East Anglia via Cambridge) before 04:30 and after 09:59, and at Stratford (London) after 09:44 and Tottenham Hale after 09:45.
Restriction.DetailsReturn:
Valid on any train departing London Liverpool Street (or London Kings Cross or passing through London St Pancras International to East Anglia via Cambridge) after 09:29, except to Ipswich and beyond (or to Brandon and beyond via Cambridge) between 16:31 and 18:35.

Valid on north- or eastbound trains departing Stratford (London) after 09:29 except to Ipswich and beyond (or to Brandon and beyond via Cambridge) between 16:36 and 18:43.

Valid on northbound trains departing Tottenham Hale after 09:29 except, to Brandon and beyond via Cambridge, between 16:41 and 18:46.

Restriction.DetailsOutward: Not valid on trains timed to arrive in London Liverpool Street or London Kings Cross after 04:29 and before 10:00.
Restriction.DetailsReturn: Not valid on trains timed to depart London Terminals after 04:29 and before 09:30

TicketRestriction.Name: 1D
TicketRestriction.Notes:
Break of journey is permitted. For long-haul train journeys, if a customer begins their journey at such a time that the timetable does not facilitate completion of the journey into their destination, customers may break their journey overnight. The same time restrictions apply for any continuation of travel on day 2 in this scenario.

Customers must not travel on CrossCountry services before 09:30.
TicketRestriction.SeasonalVariations:
Restrictions will be lifted for trains departing from and arriving at London Kings Cross and Stevenage on a Friday until further notice. 

Restrictions will be eased on Monday 24th August 2026 in both directions.

Restrictions will be eased between 21st December 2026 and 1st January 2027 in both directions.

Evening restrictions will be lifted for trains departing London Kings Cross and Stevenage on Thursday 2nd April 2026 due to the Easter Bank Holiday weekend. Restrictions will be eased between Friday 3rd April 2026 and Monday 6th April 2026 in both directions. Restrictions will be eased on Monday 4th May and 25th May 2026 in both directions.

Generally, Friday evening restriction easements apply on a Thursday before any Friday that is a Bank Holiday where travelling north from London Kings Cross or Stevenage.

TicketRestriction.SeasonalVariations:
GWR Christmas and New Year Travel Restrictions 2025/26

Up to and including Friday 19 December: Usual ticket restrictions apply.

Monday 22 to Wednesday 24 December:

Off-Peak tickets valid all day.

Super Off-Peak tickets subject to normal restriction times.

Monday 29 December to Friday 02 January * No ticket restrictions apply.

Monday 05 January 2026 onwards Usual ticket restrictions apply.

Journey planners have been updated with the above information.

*Please note this only applies to journeys priced by GWR. Flows within Wales, between Cheltenham and Gloucester and long-distance routes to areas not served by GWR are priced by other train companies, who may have different Christmas travel restrictions in place. TicketRestriction.SeasonalVariations:
GWR Christmas and New Year Travel Restrictions 2025/26

Up to and including Friday 19 December: Usual ticket restrictions apply.

Monday 22 to Wednesday 24 December:

Off-Peak tickets valid all day.

Super Off-Peak tickets subject to normal restriction times.

Monday 29 December to Friday 02 January * No ticket restrictions apply.

Monday 05 January 2026 onwards Usual ticket restrictions apply.

Journey planners have been updated with the above information.

*Please note this only applies to journeys priced by GWR. Flows within Wales, between Cheltenham and Gloucester and long-distance routes to areas not served by GWR are priced by other train companies, who may have different Christmas travel restrictions in place. Usual restrictions will apply to CPAY and may apply to other Operators’ services. Please check retail systems if unsure.

Customers using Off-Peak travel cards and arriving at London Paddington before 09:30 on weekdays, will need to wait until 09:30 for onward travel from Paddington.

Restrictions will be lifted for trains departing from London Kings Cross and Stevenage on an evening on a Friday until further notice. 

Restrictions will be eased on Monday 24th August 2026 in both directions.

Restrictions will be eased between 21st December 2026 and 1st January 2027 in both directions.

Restrictions will be eased between Friday 3rd April 2026 and Monday 6th April 2026 in both directions.

Restrictions will be eased on Monday 4th May 2026 and 25th May 2026 in both directions.

Generally, Friday evening restriction easements apply on a Thursday before any Friday that is a Bank Holiday where travelling north from London Kings Cross or Stevenage.

Usual time restrictions apply to other Train Operator's services and TfL tickets throughout unless advised otherwise.

Up to and including Friday 19 December: Usual ticket restrictions apply.

Off-Peak tickets valid all day.

Super Off-Peak tickets subject to normal restriction times.

Monday 29 December to Friday 02 January * No ticket restrictions apply.

Monday 05 January 2026 onwards Usual ticket restrictions apply.

*Please note this only applies to journeys priced by GWR. Flows within Wales, between Cheltenham and Gloucester and long-distance routes to areas not served by GWR are priced by other train companies, who may have different Christmas travel restrictions in place. Usual restrictions will apply to CPAY and may apply to other Operators’ services. Please check retail systems if unsure.

TicketRestriction.Name: 70min Flex
TicketRestriction.Notes:
If intending to make use of the flexibility offered by your ticket, it is recommended that you digitally amend your booking at www.lner.co.uk.

If unable to amend your booking, you may still travel via alternative services as defined by the validity of your ticket.

You may only travel via alternative services beyond this validity where there is ticket acceptance in place or where you have missed a connection due to earlier delays to your journey.

On-board validation equipment is programmed to assume you boarded the train at the origin of your ticket if the train serves that location. If you boarded the train later, you will need to inform onboard staff so that ticket validation returns the correct response.

Restriction.DetailsOutward:
Customers can either travel as booked, or draw on the freedom to start the journey up to 70 minutes earlier or later than originally scheduled when they first purchased the ticket.

'Break of journey' is not permitted under the terms and conditions of your ticket.

Customers must travel such that their scheduled arrival time is within 180 minutes (3 hours) of the original booked arrival time.

For full terms and conditions, see here.

Tickets are not valid with Lumo, Grand Central, Hull Trains or Avanti West Coast.
Restriction.DetailsReturn: Customers should purchase a separate single ticket for return travel.

TicketRestriction.Name: 7B
TicketRestriction.Easement:
• 08:20 Clacton-on-Sea to London Liverpool Street service - from stations Clacton to Hythe (Essex), to all destinations;
  • 08:01 Walton-on-the-Naze to London Liverpool Street service - from Walton-on-the-Naze, Frinton-on-Sea and Kirby Cross to all destinations;
  • 08:08 Harwich Town to Colchester service - from all stations to all destinations;
  • 09:02 London Liverpool Street to Ipswich service - from London Liverpool Street and Stratford (London) to Chappel & Wakes Colne, Bures and Sudbury (Suffolk) only, changing at Marks Tey;
  • 09:18 London Liverpool Street to Clacton-on-Sea service - from London Liverpool Street and Stratford (London) to Wivenhoe, Thorpe-le-Soken and Clacton-on-Sea, and by changing at Thorpe-le-Soken, to, Kirby Cross, Frinton-on-Sea and Walton-on-the-Naze;
  • 09:28 London Liverpool Street to Cambridge North service - from London Liverpool Street to all destinations.

TicketRestriction.Name: 9A
TicketRestriction.Easement:
• 08:20 Clacton-on-Sea to London Liverpool Street service - from Clacton, Thorpe-le-Soken, Great Bentley, Alresford and Wivenhoe to all destinations.
  • 08:01 Walton-on-the-Naze to London Liverpool Street service - from Walton-on-the-Naze, Frinton-on-Sea and Kirby Cross to all destinations.
  • 08:08 Harwich Town to Colchester service - from all stations to all destinations.
  • 09:02 London Liverpool Street to Ipswich service - from London Liverpool Street and Stratford (London) to Chappel & Wakes Colne, Bures and Sudbury (Suffolk) only, changing at Marks Tey.
  • 09:18 London Liverpool Street to Clacton-on-Sea service - from London Liverpool Street and Stratford (London) to  Wivenhoe, Thorpe-le-Soken, Clacton-on-Sea, Kirby Cross, Frinton-on-Sea and Walton-on-the-Naze.
  • 09:28 London Liverpool Street to Cambridge service - from London Liverpool Street to all destinations.
  • 18:30 London Liverpool Street to Norwich service - from London Liverpool Street to Derby Road (Ipswich), Trimley and Felixstowe only, changing at Ipswich.
TicketRestriction.SeasonalVariations:
School Holidays - Evening peak restrictions on outward journeys from London Liverpool Street and Stratford (London) (which only apply to Ipswich and beyond) and from Liverpool Street and Tottenham Hale (which only apply to Brandon and beyond via Cambridge) are lifted during school holidays and half-term weeks. Note that restrictions from London Kings Cross are not affected and remain in place.

2024

Summer: Monday 22 July to Tuesday 3 September

Autumn Half-Term: Friday 25 October to Friday 1 November

Christmas and New Year: Monday 23 December to Friday 3 January 2025.

2025

Half-Term: Monday 17 to Friday 21 February

Easter: Monday 7 to Tuesday 22 April (Friday 18 and Monday 21 April are public holidays)

Half-Term: Tuesday 27 to Friday 30 May (Monday 26 May is a public holiday)

Summer: Starts 23 July.

All dates are inclusive and are taken from the Norfolk County Council website.

TicketRestriction.SeasonalVariations:
Restrictions will be lifted for trains departing from and arriving at London Kings Cross and Stevenage on a Friday until further notice.

Restrictions will be eased on Monday 24th August 2026 in both directions.

Restrictions will be eased between 21st December 2026 and 1st January 2027 in both directions.

Evening restrictions will be lifted for trains departing London Kings Cross and Stevenage on Thursday 2nd April 2026 due to the Easter Bank Holiday weekend. Restrictions will be eased between Friday 3rd April 2026 and Monday 6th April 2026 in both directions.

Restrictions will be eased on Monday 4th May 2026 and 25th May 2026 in both directions.

Generally, Friday evening restriction easements apply on a Thursday before any Friday that is a Bank Holiday where travelling north from London Kings Cross or Stevenage.

TicketRestriction.Name: C7
TicketRestriction.SeasonalVariations:
GWR Christmas and New Year Travel Restrictions 2025/26

Up to and including Friday 19 December: Usual ticket restrictions apply.

Monday 22 to Wednesday 24 December:

Off-Peak tickets valid all day.

Super Off-Peak tickets subject to normal restriction times.

Monday 29 December to Friday 02 January * No ticket restrictions apply.

Monday 05 January 2026 onwards Usual ticket restrictions apply.

Journey planners have been updated with the above information.

*Please note this only applies to journeys priced by GWR. Flows within Wales, between Cheltenham and Gloucester and long-distance routes to areas not served by GWR are priced by other train companies, who may have different Christmas travel restrictions in place. Usual restrictions will apply to CPAY and may apply to other Operators’ services. Please check retail systems if unsure.

Customers using Off-Peak travel cards and arriving at London Paddington before 09:30 on weekdays, will need to wait until 09:30 for onward travel from Paddington.

Discounted child tickets are available to children aged 5-15 years.

Restriction.DetailsOutward:
Any ticket using this restriction code (DB) must not be sold from any rail station ticket office, travel agents or thetrainline or any other online ticket issuing system.

Tickets using this restriction code (DB) can only be sold by the Centro Sales Team based at Centro House, Birmingham. These tickets are offered to members of their Corporate Discount Scheme and to passengers who purchase their season ticket by Direct Debit.

Customers with enquiries should contact the Centro Sales Team on 0121 214 7088.
Restriction.DetailsReturn:
Any ticket using this restriction code (DB) must not be sold from any rail station ticket office, travel agents or thetrainline or any other online ticket issuing system.

Customers with enquiries should contact the Centro Sales Team on 0121 214 7088.

Restriction.DetailsOutward:

Restriction.DetailsReturn:

TicketRestriction.Name: DF

It is valid on all train companies services that operate within the NWM area for an unlimited number of journeys on the date printed on the ticket. The Family ticket is available for groups of up to 6 people. At least 1 but no more than 2 must be adults, children are classed as aged 5-15 years inclusive. Each Family group must contain at least 1 adult and 1 child.

TicketRestriction.Easement:
Theale to Westbury Closure Monday 11 to Thursday 14 November 2024

and

Newbury to Bedwyn Closure Tuesday 10 to Thursday 12 December 2024

The following timing easements will apply during these line closures, for customers travellingto/from stations (and branch lines) from Pewsey to Penzance inclusive, for journeys to / from / via Didcot, Reading, London and Southeast:

Travel from London

The 08:57 service from London Paddington to Paignton temporarily becomes Off-Peak

The 17:36 service from London Paddington to Penzance temporarily becomes Off-Peak for customers travelling to Newton Abbot / Crediton and beyond.

Plymouth to Liskeard Closure Monday 2 to Friday 6 December 2024

The 08:03 service from London Paddington to Plymouth temporarily becomesOff-Peakfor customers travelling to Menheniot, Liskeard, Bodmin and stations on the Looe Branch, only.

The 09:04 service from London Paddington to Plymouth temporarily becomesOff-Peak forcustomers travelling to Menheniot and beyond.

The 17:04 service from London Paddington to Plymouth also temporarily becomesOff-Peak for customers travelling to Menheniot and beyond.

Travel Towards London and the Southeast

Easementsallow earlier travel on trains and buses from Cornwall, to maintain connections with the usual Off-Peak services at Plymouth.

Restriction.DetailsOutward:
Not valid on trains timed to depart London Euston before 09:15 or London St Pancras International before 09:00.

The n-Network Super Off-Peak (evening single/return fare) is a flat fare generally available after 18:30 but on this journey an easement applies allowing travel from 18:22 within the n-Network (Network West Midlands PTE) area.

It is available:

  • Between any two stations in the Network West Midlands area but only after 18:30 Mondays to Sundays.
  • Return journeys must be made the same evening as the outward journey.
  • Available to adults, children and groups of 4 (children will be allowed as a part of the group as long as at least one adult is in the group). The group must travel together as only one ticket will be issued.
  • This fare is not available to unaccompanied children.

Period of validity as per the date of travel shown on the ticket.

n-Network Super Off-Peak fares are available from staffed Ticket Offices and on-train when the customer has boarded at an unstaffed station.

n-Network Super Off-Peak fares cannot be purchased for travel on the last running rail service of the day.

No Railcard discounts allowed.
Restriction.DetailsReturn:
Not valid on trains timed to depart London Euston before 09:15 or London St Pancras International before 09:00.

The n-Network Super Off-Peak (evening single/return fare) is a flat fare generally available after 18:30 but on this journey an easement applies allowing travel from 18:22 within the n-Network (Network West Midlands PTE) area.

It is available:

  • Between any two stations in the Network West Midlands area but only after 18:30 Mondays to Sundays.
  • Return journeys must be made the same evening as the outward journey.
  • Available to adults, children and groups of 4 (children will be allowed as a part of the group as long as at least one adult is in the group). The group must travel together as only one ticket will be issued.
  • This fare is not available to unaccompanied children.

Period of validity as per the date of travel shown on the ticket.

n-Network Super Off-Peak fares are available from staffed Ticket Offices and on-train when the customer has boarded at an unstaffed station.

n-Network Super Off-Peak fares cannot be purchased for travel on the last running rail service of the day.

No Railcard discounts allowed.

Restriction.DetailsOutward:

TicketRestriction.Name: FK
TicketRestriction.Notes:
If intending to make use of the flexibility offered by your ticket, it is recommended that you digitally amend your booking at www.lner.co.uk.

If unable to amend your booking, you may still travel via alternative services as defined by the validity of your ticket.

You may only travel via alternative services beyond this validity where there is ticket acceptance in place or where you have missed a connection due to earlier delays to your journey.

On-board validation equipment is programmed to assume you boarded the train at the origin of your ticket if the train serves that location. If you boarded the train later, you will need to inform onboard staff so that ticket validation returns the correct response.

Restriction.DetailsOutward:
Customers can either travel as booked, or draw on the freedom to start the journey up to 70 minutes earlier or later than originally scheduled when they first purchased the ticket.

'Break of journey' is not permitted under the terms and conditions of your ticket.

Customers must travel such that their scheduled arrival time is within 180 minutes (3 hours) of the original booked arrival time.

For full terms and conditions, see here.

Tickets are not valid with Lumo, Grand Central, Hull Trains or Avanti West Coast.
Restriction.DetailsReturn: Customers should purchase a separate single ticket for return travel.

Up to and including Friday 19 December: Usual ticket restrictions apply.

Off-Peak tickets valid all day.

Monday 29 December to Friday 02 January * No ticket restrictions apply.

Monday 05 January 2026 onwards Usual ticket restrictions apply.

*Please note this only applies to journeys priced by GWR. Flows within Wales, between Cheltenham and Gloucester and long-distance routes to areas not served by GWR are priced by other train companies, who may have different Christmas travel restrictions in place. Usual restrictions will apply to CPAY and may apply to other Operators’ services. Please check retail systems if unsure.

Up to and including Friday 19 December: Usual ticket restrictions apply.

Monday 22 to Wednesday 24 December:

Off-Peak tickets valid all day.

Super Off-Peak tickets subject to normal restriction times.

Monday 29 December to Friday 02 January * No ticket restrictions apply.

Monday 05 January 2026 onwards Usual ticket restrictions apply.

Journey planners have been updated with the above information.

*Please note this only applies to journeys priced by GWR. Flows within Wales, between Cheltenham and Gloucester and long-distance routes to areas not served by GWR are priced by other train companies, who may have different Christmas travel restrictions in place. Usual restrictions will apply to CPAY and may apply to other Operators’ services. Please check retail systems if unsure.

Up to and including Friday 19 December: Usual ticket restrictions apply.

Monday 22 to Wednesday 24 December:

Off-Peak tickets valid all day.

Super Off-Peak tickets subject to normal restriction times.

Monday 29 December to Friday 02 January * No ticket restrictions apply.

Monday 05 January 2026 onwards Usual ticket restrictions apply.

Journey planners have been updated with the above information.

*Please note this only applies to journeys priced by GWR. Flows within Wales, between Cheltenham and Gloucester and long-distance routes to areas not served by GWR are priced by other train companies, who may have different Christmas travel restrictions in place. Usual restrictions will apply to CPAY and may apply to other Operators’ services. Please check retail systems if unsure.

Restriction.DetailsOutward:
Mums Go Free tickets provide free travel for the holder when travelling with a child of any age. Proof of age or status not required. 

This offer is only available on Sunday 22 March 2020.

This ticket is only available from stations or on-train if travelling from an unstaffed station.  Not available online or as an m-Ticket or e-Ticket. 

Offer only available on selected journeys on the West Midlands Railway network.

TicketRestriction.Name: M6
TicketRestriction.SeasonalVariations:
Restrictions will be eased on Monday 24th August 2026 in both directions.

Restrictions will be lifted between 21st December 2026 and 1st January 2027 in both directions.

Restrictions will be eased between Friday 3rd April 2026 and Monday 6th April 2026 in both directions.

Restrictions will be eased on Monday 4th May 2026 and Monday 25 May 2026 in both directions.

Restriction.DetailsOutward:
Not valid for travel on services timed to arrive London Terminals before 13:00.

Not valid for travel on services timed to depart London Euston before 10:30.

Not valid for travel on services timed to depart the origin station on the ticket after 16:29 and before 19:31 in either direction.

Break of journey is not permitted except to change trains at an intermediate station or to access station facilities.

Not valid for travel on any service on Fridays, Saturdays and Sundays.

Restriction.DetailsOutward:
The n-Network add-on season ticket can only be used for travel when an accompanying valid n-Network ticket is held.

The n-Network add-on provides rail travel only from selected stations to the nearest point in the West Midlands county by any permitted route.

The ticket restrictions applicable to the n-Network ticket also apply to the n-Network add-on.

n-Network Add-on tickets are not valid in conjunction with n-Train tickets.

It is valid for travel:

  • On all train companies' services on the permitted routes.
  • Using the same restrictions applicable to the corresponding n-Network ticket.
  • On rail services only.
  • All day during the period of validity.

Available from selected stations in the n-Network.
Restriction.DetailsReturn:
The n-Network add-on season ticket can only be used for travel when an accompanying valid n-Network ticket is held.

The n-Network add-on provides rail travel only from selected stations to the nearest point in the West Midlands county by any permitted route.

n-Network Add-on tickets are not valid in conjunction with n-Train tickets.

It is valid for travel:

  • On all train companies' services on the permitted routes.
  • Using the same restrictions applicable to the corresponding n-Network ticket.
  • On rail services only.
  • All day during the period of validity.

Available from selected stations in the n-Network.

Restriction.DetailsOutward:
Not valid on trains timed to depart after 04:29 and before 09:30 or after 16:00 and before 18:30.

The restriction after 16:00 and before 18:30 includes any connecting train departing during this period (or re-commencing your journey following a break of journey) within the following areas:

  • Transport for Greater Manchester area plus Alderley Edge, Birchwood, Burscough Bridge, Disley,  Handforth, Hoscar, New Mills Central, New Mills Newtown, Padgate, Parbold, Styal, Warrington Bank Quay, Warrington Central, and Wilmslow;
  • Travel South Yorkshire area plus, Darton, Denby Dale, Moorthorpe and South Elmsall;
  • West Yorkshire Metro area plus Cononley, Harrogate,  Hornbeam Park, Knaresborough, Pannal, Skipton, Starbeck and Weeton.

Valid trains can also be checked using the journey planner at www.nationalrail.co.uk

Further information including maps of the restricted areas can be found on the Northern website.

Valid trains can also be checked using the journey planner at www.nationalrail.co.uk

Further information including maps of the restricted areas can be found on the Northern website.

07:41 from Heyford

07:45 from Tackley

Not valid for change of trains or break of journey between Heyford / Tackley and London Paddington. Boarding anywhere other than Heyford or Tackley with tickets from Heyford or Tackley is not permitted on this service. If Off-peak tickets from Heyford or Tackley include a travelcard, the travelcard element will not be valid for onward travel from London Paddington on the underground, buses or Elizabeth Line, until 09:30.

All times are inclusive

GWR Christmas and New Year Travel Restrictions 2025/26

Up to and including Friday 19 December: Usual ticket restrictions apply.

Monday 22 to Wednesday 24 December:

Off-Peak tickets valid all day.

Super Off-Peak tickets subject to normal restriction times.

Monday 29 December to Friday 02 January * No ticket restrictions apply.

Monday 05 January 2026 onwards Usual ticket restrictions apply.

Journey planners have been updated with the above information.

*Please note this only applies to journeys priced by GWR. Flows within Wales, between Cheltenham and Gloucester and long-distance routes to areas not served by GWR are priced by other train companies, who may have different Christmas travel restrictions in place. Usual restrictions will apply to CPAY and may apply to other Operators’ services. Please check retail systems if unsure.

Restriction.DetailsOutward:
Fare charged is based on the time of touch in on the gate or card validator at the origin station of the journey:

Mondays-Fridays peak fare applies between 06:30-09:30 and 16:00-19:00. Not applicable all day Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.

You must touch in and out with a contactless payment card or contactless device.

You can also use an Oyster card with pay as you go credit, but only for travel between stations in London Zones 1-9, Bayford, Broxbourne, Chafford Hundred, Cuffley, Earlswood, Epsom, Gatwick Airport, Grays, Herford East, Hertford North, Horley, Merstham Ockendon, Potters Bar, Purfleet, Radlett, Redhill, Rye House, Salfords, St Margarets, Ware, Watford Junction.

Charges capped at daily price cap limit, based on all journeys made on the same card or device between 04:30 and 04:29 the following day.

Fare charged is based on the time of touch in on the gate or card card validator at the origin station of the journey:

Mondays-Fridays off-peak fare not valid between 06:30-09:30 and 16:00-19:00. Valid all day Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.

You must touch in and out with a contactless payment card or contactless device.

You can also use an Oyster card with pay as you go credit, but only for travel between stations in London Zones 1-9, Bayford, Broxbourne, Chafford Hundred, Cuffley, Earlswood, Epsom, Gatwick Airport, Grays, Hertford East, Hertford North, Horley, Merstham Ockendon, Potters Bar, Purfleet, Radlett, Redhill, Rye House, Salfords, St Margarets, Ware, Watford Junction.

Charges capped at daily price cap limit, based on all journeys made on all journeys made on the same card or device between 04:30 and 04:29 the following day.

TicketRestriction.Name: W1
TicketRestriction.SeasonalVariations:
GWR Christmas and New Year Travel Restrictions 2025/26

Up to and including Friday 19 December: Usual ticket restrictions apply.

Monday 22 to Wednesday 24 December:

Off-Peak tickets valid all day.

Super Off-Peak tickets subject to normal restriction times.

Monday 29 December to Friday 02 January * No ticket restrictions apply.

Monday 05 January 2026 onwards Usual ticket restrictions apply.

Journey planners have been updated with the above information.

*Please note this only applies to journeys priced by GWR. Flows within Wales, between Cheltenham and Gloucester and long-distance routes to areas not served by GWR are priced by other train companies, who may have different Christmas travel restrictions in place. Usual restrictions will apply to CPAY and may apply to other Operators’ services. Please check retail systems if unsure.

Customers using Off-Peak travel cards and arriving at London Paddington before 09:30 on weekdays, will need to wait until 09:30 for onward travel from Paddington.
